interaction_phrase: according to the Australian Broadcasting Corporation
occurrence_sentence: Chinese scientists have transmitted the quantum version of computer memory between two entangled clouds of atoms over 50 kilometers, according to the Australian Broadcasting Corporation.
Chinese scientists say the experiment, published today in the journal [Nature](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-020-1976-7), is a step towards building a quantum internet.

While the achievement does not mean that people will be tapping into a [quantum internet](https://thequantuminsider.com/2019/09/21/g-force-or-the-quantum-internet/) anytime soon, it is another significant step into the quantum era. — [Genelle Weule](https://www.abc.net.au/news/genelle-weule/7098742)

interaction_phrase: Matt Swayne has worked as a science communicator for an R1 university for more than 12 years, specializing in translating high tech and deep tech for the general audience. He has served as a writer, editor and analyst at The Quantum Insider since its inception.
occurrence_sentence: With a several-decades long background in journalism and communications, Matt Swayne has worked as a science communicator for an R1 university for more than 12 years, specializing in translating high tech and deep tech for the general audience.
